subject: [PATCH v5 26/36] mm: add NODE_PRIVATE_CAP_RECLAIM for opted-in private node reclaim

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a new mechanism to opt private nodes into the reclaim process, allowing them to participate in memory management and compaction. The `NODE_PRIVATE_CAP_RECLAIM` flag is introduced to control this behavior, which can be set by drivers for specific workloads. This change enables private nodes to use normal watermarks and allows swap-out operations, while excluding cross-node operations like demotion and promotion. The patch also updates various kernel modules to accommodate the new functionality.

reviews.author: Richard Cheng
reviews.summary: Reviewer Richard Cheng noted that shrink_lruvec() in mem_cgroup_shrink_node() directly calls shrink_lruvec() without passing through shrink_node(), which bypasses the NODE_PRIVATE_CAP_RECLAIM check, and requested an explanation for why this path is allowed to reclaim private nodes.
reviews.raw_body:
I applied this code change and notice in mem_cgroup_shrink_node(), it calls
shrink_lruvec() directly and never passes through shrink_node().

This patch makes private nodes reachable through memcg reclaim zonelist, but
what prevents the direct per-node memcg path from reclaiming a private node
that has not set NODE_PRIVATE_CAP_RECLAIM ?

--Richard

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that they missed implementing NODE_PRIVATE_CAP_RECLAIM in vmscan.c and promises to fix it in v6, also expressing interest in simplifying the interaction between mm/ sub-components.
reviews.raw_body:
I legitimately missed this.

Sashiko also picked it up on the filter patch:
https://sashiko.dev/#/message/20260721183528.B4B9C1F00A3A%40smtp.kernel.org

If nothing else - implementing this feature has taught me some
sub-components of mm/ have very well centralized logic, and others (like
vmscan.c) have 3 or 4 different paths in with a lot of strange quirks
carved out.

I think some of the vmscan paths can be improved to simplify this
interaction, but i will fix this particular miss in v6 and come back
around to see whether we can clean up these different paths as a general
improvement.

~Gregory

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch adds a mechanism to opt private nodes into the reclaim process, allowing them to participate in memory reclamation operations such as kswapd and direct reclaim. The patch introduces a new capability called NODE_PRIVATE_CAP_RECLAIM that can be set by drivers for private nodes to enable or disable specific services like compaction, madvise, and DAMON. This allows for more fine-grained control over memory management on private nodes, preventing unpredictable behavior in certain workflows. The patch also updates various kernel modules to use the new capability and handle node-private reclaim accordingly.
